step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the previous assessment. Myositis ossificans specifically demonstrates dystrophic calcification through its hallmark feature of heterotopic bone formation within muscles following injury or chronic irritation. This process fits precisely into the definition of dystrophic calcification where mineralization occurs secondary to cell death or tissue damage without an associated vascular lesion. Thus, maintaining consistency with the earlier conclusion, the correct answer remains Option D: Myositis ossificans. Final
